results.detailed_description: Done. The Discover feed no longer scrolls you into a new question on its own. Before, when a question's last argument finished, the feed rolled straight into the next recommended question and started reading it aloud (and you could also swipe into it). I removed the next-question card that used to sit at the bottom of each question's feed, so now the feed simply stops on the current question's last argument and holds there. To go to a different question you open the Browse questions button in the toolbar (the speech-bubble icon) and pick one — exactly as you asked. I kept the hands-free playback *within* a question: it still reads the question, then plays through its arguments one after another without you touching the screen; it just doesn't jump to a new question at the end anymore. I couldn't run the app to see it live (builds/deploys happen after hand-off), so this is verified by reading the code path, not by driving the UI. If you actually meant you want NO auto-advance at all — not even between arguments in the same question — say so and it's a one-line follow-up.
results.identified_issue: The Discover feed appended the next recommended question's card as the last page of the current question's feed (DiscoverView.pages). When the last argument finished, the hands-free advance (advanceToNextPage) — and equally a manual swipe — landed on that trailing card, and handleLanding then called select(completingCurrent:true) to hand the listener into a brand-new question. I removed the trailing next-question card from pages so the feed simply ends on the current question's last clip; that kills both the automatic roll-over and the swipe-into-next-question handoff in one place. Deleted the now-dead nextQuestion computed property and the dead handoff branch in handleLanding. I deliberately KEPT within-question hands-free advance (question card -> clip1 -> clip2 ...), since the request was specifically about reaching a NEW question. If instead you want ALL hands-free auto-advance gone (even clip-to-clip within a question), the one-line follow-up is removing the scheduleAdvance(afterHearing:) call. completedQuestionIDs/completingCurrent are left in place as harmless persisted state.
results.summary: Removed the auto-scroll into the next question — new questions now come only from the Browse questions tab

description: Let's remove the auto scroll to next question feature. In order to go to a next question to a new question we should have to go open the question tab.
